questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The shortest wavelength of the Brakcet sereis of a hydrogen-like atom (atomic number $=\mathrm{z}$ ) is the same as the shortest wavelength of the Balmer series of hydrogen atom. Find the value of $z$.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{E} \propto \frac{\mathrm{Z}^2}{\mathrm{n}^2}$
In series limit of bracket axis, transition is for $n=\infty$ to $n=4$
In series limit of Balmer sereis, transition for $n=\infty$ to $n=2$
$\mathrm{h} v_{\text {bracket }}=13.6 \mathrm{Z}^2\left[\frac{1}{16}\right] \Rightarrow \mathrm{h} v_{\text {Balmer }}=13.6\left[\frac{1}{4}\right]$
$\frac{13.6 Z^2}{16}=\frac{13.6}{4}=Z=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a hydrogen like atom whose energy in $n^{\text {th }}$ excited state is given by
$E_n=-\frac{13.6}{n^2} Z^2$
when this excited atom makes a transition from an excited state to ground state. The most energetic photons have energy $\mathrm{E}_{\max }=52.224 \mathrm{eV}$ and the least energetic photons have energy $\mathrm{E}_{\min }=1.224 \mathrm{eV}$. Find the atomic number of atom.
questions.solution:
Max" energy is liberated for transition
$E_n \rightarrow E_1$
and minimum energy for $E_n \rightarrow E_{n-1}$
Hence,
$\frac{E_1}{n^2}-\frac{E_1}{12}=52.224 \mathrm{eV}$
and $\quad \frac{E_1}{n^2}-\frac{E_1}{(n-1)^2}=1.224 \mathrm{eV}$
Solving we get,
$E_1=-54.4 \mathrm{eV}$
and $\quad n=5$
hence,
$E_1=-\frac{13.6 Z^2}{12}=-54.4$
$Z=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
